title: acinos arvensis subsp. villosus
intro: Acinos arvensis subsp. villosus, also known as Woolly Thyme or Woolly Mint, is a subspecies of perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its woolly leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Acinos arvensis subsp. villosus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties against certain pathogens. Herbal preparations of Woolly Thyme include tea infusions, essential oil extracts, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
